text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

20,000 police troops and security personnel will be in place in the Saint-Denis area of Paris ahead of the last day of the Olympics

A shirtless man attempted to scale the Eiffel Tower, prompting authorities to evacuate the Paris landmark only hours before theOlympics closing ceremony.

The man was seen climbing the330-metre toweron Sunday afternoon but French police said they “immediately intervened and arrested” him.

Police escorted visitors away from the area at around 3 pm.

In one video posted to social media, he can be seen smiling, saying to bystanders “Bloody warm, innit?” in what appears to be a British accent, as he is escorted off the viewing platform by police.

Videos posted on social media showed the rogue climber navigating the Olympic rings.

It was unclear where he began his ascent but he was spotted above the Olympic rings sitting on the structure, just above the first viewing deck.

Police escorted visitors away from the area and the entry beneath the tower was closed. Some visitors were briefly locked in on the second floor but were allowed to exit around 30 minutes later.

A spokesperson for Paris police said: “An individual started climbing the Eiffel Tower at 2.45pm, police intervened and the person was detained.”

More than 30,000 police officers have been deployed around Paris with France’s Interior Minister Gerald Darmanin saying about 3,000 police officers will be mobilised around the Stade de France.

The closing ceremony will begin at the Stade de France in the Saint-Denis area, a distance away from the tower, at around 9pm.

Impartial news + intelligent debate

title: Eiffel Tower evacuated as man seen scaling landmark hours before closing ceremony
author: Emilia Randall
description: 20,000 police troops and security personnel will be in place in the Saint-Denis area of Paris ahead of the last day of the Olympics
